outcome_contract.why: Drift between near-duplicate screens is compounding silently; attribution data is already missing from apartment bookings, and visual inconsistencies are live. Fixing now prevents further compounding before the next feature layer touches these screens.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Both booking screens use the canonical analytics pattern (attribution present on all handoffs); visual tokens are consistent; double-room has component-level tests matching apartment coverage.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Both screens render NR/Flex booking cards; visual tokens diverged (brand-accent vs brand-primary for NR card, badge text, dark mode border)
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Token drift is live; NR button on apartment renders with accent colour instead of primary

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Pax state, date state, long-stay flag — apartment only. isValidRange gating on both.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No UX regression risk from analytics change or token fix

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No auth, no PII beyond what's already in URL params.
GA4 events via `fireHandoffToEngine` (apartment) and `trackThenNavigate` (double-room). Attribution data absent from apartment handoffs.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Attribution gap is the primary business risk — apartment bookings cannot be attributed to source.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Apartment: 2 test files. Double-room: 0 test files.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Double-room has no tests; apartment test must be updated after analytics migration.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — add double-room tests, update apartment test

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: GA4 event payload contracts. `sessionStorage` apartment key.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Must not drop existing GA4 fields when migrating analytics call.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Beacon transport confirmed on both: `fireHandoffToEngine` uses beacon; `trackThenNavigate.ts:69` explicitly passes `transport_type: "beacon"` with 200ms timeout fallback and navigation callback.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No performance gap — migration is transport-equivalent.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No feature flag. Change is atomic per-component.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Simple code change; rollback = revert commit. Low risk.

stage_payload.scope_summary: Two private-room booking screens — `ApartmentBookContent.tsx` and `DoubleRoomBookContent.tsx` — share ~85% structural overlap but have accumulated silent drift divergences over time without any shared abstraction or review mechanism. The most impactful divergence is the analytics layer: the apartment screen uses the deprecated `fireHandoffToEngine` call while the double-room screen uses the newer `trackThenNavigate` + `readAttribution` pattern, meaning apartment handoffs currently send no attribution data. Additional drift includes visual token inconsistencies, a missing test suite on double-r
goals: Identify and document all confirmed drift divergences between the two booking screens.
Fix the analytics attribution gap on `ApartmentBookContent` (highest business impact).
Correct visual token divergences so both screens render consistently.
Add component-level tests for `DoubleRoomBookContent` to close the coverage gap.
non_goals: Full shared-base component extraction (deferred — intentional differences are non-trivial).
Unifying pax handling, rate codes, or room-specific copy.
Adding a general-purpose "duplicate screen detector" to CI (future Ideas candidate, not now).
constraints: Must not break existing GA4 event contracts. Apartment analytics events must preserve all existing fields; only the call pattern changes.
`fireHandoffToEngine` in `ga4-events.ts` must remain (other callers may exist).
No i18n changes — namespaces differ intentionally.
assumptions: The `trackThenNavigate` + `readAttribution` pattern in `DoubleRoomBookContent` is the canonical pattern going forward, based on code evidence.
`isLongStay` is apartment-specific behaviour; it is intentional that double-room does not have it.
Visual token drift (`brand-accent` vs `brand-primary` for NR button, badge text colour, dark mode border) are bugs, not intentional design differences.
